page: Amazon Web Services f2.6xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.4 GHz. It features 24 vCPUs across 12 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 10.67 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provided via a 940 GB NVMe SSD, and network connectivity is supported by a 12 Gbps baseline bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core tests. Multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall within the average range. The server demonstrates strong performance in text generation for small language models, though it exhibits weak to poor performance when processing larger 70B parameter models due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the f2.6xlarge offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and fast local NVMe storage, making it suitable for memory-intensive workloads without the premium of GPU-accelerated hardware. This profile is well-suited for database hosting, memory-cached applications, software compilation, and medium-scale data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services f2.6xlarge is an x86_64 server featuring a dedicated AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 24 vCPUs, 12 physical cores, and a 3.4 GHz clock speed. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory and a 940 GB local NVMe SSD, supported by a 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance and efficient text generation for small language models, while multi-core operations, memory bandwidth, and database tasks perform at average levels. Lacking a GPU, it struggles with large-scale model inference. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio and fast local storage make this instance highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and software compilation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services f2.6xlarge is an x86_64 server featuring 24 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 940 GB NVMe SSD. It delivers strong single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads and databases.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services f2.6xlarge is an x86_64 server with 24 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and a 940 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-memory x86_64 server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and local NVMe storage for memory-intensive database and compilation workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 24 vCPUs and 3.4 GHz clock speed
256 GB DDR4 memory providing a high memory-to-core ratio
940 GB local NVMe SSD storage for low-latency I/O operations
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Strong single-core CPU and small-model text generation performance
Best suited for databases, software compilation, and memory-cached applications
